core.note: All 11 core courses are required (BIA 580, FIN 515, MIS 631, MIS 632, MIS 633, MIS 634, MIS 637, BIA 650, BIA 652, BIA 654, BIA 686)

concentration_electives.note: Choose 3 courses from exactly one concentration: Data Analytics, Data Science and AI, or Big Data

concentration_specific_rules.big_data: BIA 678 (Big Data Technologies) is mandatory; choose 2 additional from the Data Science and AI concentration electives
concentration_specific_rules.data_analytics: Choose any 3 from the Data Analytics concentration elective pool
concentration_specific_rules.data_science_and_ai: Choose any 3 from the Data Science and AI concentration elective pool

degree_requirements.notes: Students must maintain a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 to graduate

core.description: Required for all students (11 courses, 33 credits)

courses.name: Foundations of Business Analytics
courses.description: Prepares students for advanced business analytics by covering major mathematical and statistical concepts including Linear Algebra, Differential Calculus, and Probability and Statistics. Bridges theory and practice through business applications drawn from Digital Marketing, Finance, and Economics. Python is utilized throughout for examples and assignments.

courses.name: Financial Decision Making
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Session 1
courses.description: Surveys accounting and finance methods to equip technical executives with skills for effective decisions that maximize shareholder value. Bridges accounting analysis of past performance with financial projections of future economic impacts.
courses.prerequisites: Graduate student status or at least junior standing

courses.name: Data Management
courses.typically_offered: Must be taken with MIS 632 in same semester
courses.description: Focuses on data and database management, with emphasis on modeling and design and their application to business decision making. Provides conceptual understanding of both organizational and technical issues associated with data. Topics include normalization, entity-relationship modeling, relational database design, SQL, DDL, master data management, NoSQL databases (MongoDB), and cloud computing.

courses.name: Data Management LAB
courses.typically_offered: Must be taken with MIS 631 in same semester
courses.description: Provides hands-on experience in designing, implementing, and querying databases using ERWIN (data modeling), PostgreSQL, SQL, and MongoDB.

courses.name: Business Intelligence & Data Integration
courses.typically_offered: Must be taken with MIS 634 in same semester
courses.description: Focuses on the design and management of data warehouse (DW) and business intelligence (BI) systems. Organized around: business value of data, planning and business requirements, architecture, data design, implementation, BI, deployment, data integration, and emerging issues.

courses.name: Business Intelligence & Data Integration - LAB
courses.typically_offered: Must be taken with MIS 633 in same semester
courses.description: Provides hands-on experience in designing, implementing, and querying data warehouses and large-scale database systems using Alteryx (ETL tool), ERWIN (data modeling), and MongoDB.

courses.name: Data Analytics and Machine Learning
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Session 1
courses.description: Examines Data Mining and Knowledge Discovery Algorithms and their applications in solving real world business and operation problems. Covers data preprocessing, k-nearest neighborhood algorithms, machine learning and decision trees, artificial neural networks, clustering, and evaluation techniques.

courses.name: Optimization and Process Analytics
courses.description: Addresses basic concepts in optimization and heuristic search with emphasis on process improvement. Students apply mathematical optimization models to healthcare, logistics, supply chain management, capital budgeting, and portfolio analysis. Uses Microsoft Excel's Solver add-on.

courses.name: Multivariate Data Analysis I
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Session 1
courses.description: Introduces multivariate analysis methods through R programming. Students master techniques including principal components analysis, factor analysis, structural equation modeling, multidimensional scaling, correspondence analysis, cluster analysis, MANOVA, discriminant function analysis, and logistic regression. Emphasizes data visualization and presenting analytical findings.

courses.name: Experimental Design II
courses.description: Addresses core experimentation concepts including hypothesis creation, operational definitions, reliability and validity, and measurement techniques. Covers design methodologies such as sampling, randomization, and counterbalancing. Students complete a capstone project involving research question development, experimental design, data collection and analysis.
courses.prerequisites: MGT 620 or equivalent; basic knowledge of statistics expected

courses.name: Practicum in Analytics
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Session 1
courses.description: Emphasizes leveraging business intelligence and analytics for effective decision-making. Students apply concepts, principles, and methods associated with four areas of analytics (texts, descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive) to real problems in an application domain associated with their area of interest.

data_analytics_concentration_electives.description: Choose 3 courses for Data Analytics Concentration

courses.name: Social Network Analytics and Visualization
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Session 1
courses.description: Introduces foundational concepts in social network analysis with practical applications. Explores graph-theoretic fundamentals, centrality, cohesion, affiliations, equivalence, and roles. Examines marketing applications including customer profiling, community detection, targeting, sentiment analysis, and development of recommendation systems.
courses.prerequisites: Not explicitly listed; requires prior R, Python, machine learning knowledge

courses.name: Management of AI Technologies
courses.description: Examines how artificial intelligence applications function across business contexts. Covers autonomous transportation, fraud detection, machine translation, meeting scheduling, and face recognition. Focuses on management considerations including fairness, accountability, transparency, ethics, and legal implications within AI projects.

courses.name: Marketing Analytics
courses.description: Explores marketing analytics techniques including segmentation, positioning, and forecasting. Emphasizes real-world application through case studies and actual company data. Students analyze datasets and develop strategic recommendations based on their findings.

courses.name: Supply Chain Analytics
courses.description: Explores data-driven decision-making in supply chain management. Addresses strategic and operational aspects including supplier analytics, capacity planning, demand-supply matching, sales and operations planning, location analysis, network management, inventory management, and sourcing. Covers predictive, descriptive, and prescriptive analytics methodologies.

data_science_and_ai_concentration_electi.description: Choose 3 courses for Data Science and AI Concentration

courses.name: Advanced Data Analytics and Machine Learning
courses.description: Teaches systematic analytical approaches to corporate data through statistical learning algorithms. Covers time series and statistical and graphical models used for inference and prediction, with applications in finance, marketing, operations, and biomedicine.
courses.prerequisites: Graduate student status AND completion of BIA 652 or MGT 620; foundational knowledge in probability theory and linear algebra

courses.name: Web Mining
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Session 1
courses.description: Teaches students to extract data from the web and analyze web-scale data using distributed computing. Explores analytical methods used by major tech companies and startups, culminating in applying these techniques to address research questions or develop web applications.

courses.name: Data Stream Analytics
courses.description: Introduces the emerging field of data streams analytics, addressing challenges posed by continuously growing datasets from sensors, RFID tags, and smart devices. Explores IoT architecture, data quality issues, and methodologies for analyzing real-time data. Topics include synopsis and sampling techniques, sliding windows, computing entropy in streams, data stream correlations, change detection, outliers and anomaly detection.

courses.name: Big Data Technologies
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Session 1
courses.description: Addresses the emerging field of Big Data through established BI techniques and innovative approaches to managing data's volume, velocity, and variety. Covers technological, managerial, governance, and organizational dimensions of data-centric work.
courses.prerequisites: Prior experience in multivariate statistics, data mining, data management, and programming

courses.name: Big Data Practicum
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Semester
courses.description: Trains students to think critically about the process, tools, techniques, technologies, and governance for an entire data pipeline, from data through application. Students work with real-world data and business scenarios to create case studies, develop complete data pipelines, and document projects with detailed reports.
courses.prerequisites: One of: MIS 631, MIS 632, MIS 633, MIS 634, MIS 637, or BIA 652 — OR instructor permission

big_data_concentration_required.description: BIA 678 is required for Big Data Concentration; select 2 additional from Data Science and AI electives
big_data_concentration_required.rule_note: Select BIA 678 plus any 2 courses from data_science_and_ai_concentration_electives to fulfill the Big Data concentration (3 courses total)

courses.name: Big Data Technologies
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Session 1
courses.description: Addresses the emerging field of Big Data through established BI techniques and innovative approaches to managing data's volume, velocity, and variety. Covers technological, managerial, governance, and organizational dimensions of data-centric work.
courses.prerequisites: Prior experience in multivariate statistics, data mining, data management, and programming

additional_electives.description: Additional elective courses available for selection

courses.name: Augmented Intelligence and Generative AI
courses.description: Examines cognitive computing and its implications for big data analytics and evidence-based decision making. Topics include cognitive computing design principles, natural language processing, knowledge representation, advanced analytics, IBM Watson DeepQA, and Google TensorFlow deep learning frameworks. Students develop cognitive applications and analyze how knowledge-based AI and deep learning influence data science.

courses.name: Management of the Data Resource
courses.description: Designed to explain and demystify big data in non-technical terms, bridging the gap between market buzz and business realities. Examines practical implementations and financial returns on big data investments, analyzes both successful and unsuccessful applications, and reduces complex concepts to essential managerial knowledge for informed decision-making.

courses.name: Introduction to Deep Learning and Business Applications
courses.description: Introduces deep learning fundamentals tailored for business students. Progresses from basic constructs of neural networks through advanced models including convolutional neural networks, recurrent networks, generative models, and reinforcement learning. Examines potential implications and risks of applying deep learning in the business world.

courses.name: Risk Management and Simulation
courses.description: Explores theoretical and practical aspects of risk assessment and management. Covers innovation in competitive environments, uncertainty analysis, decision trees, binomial methods, Black-Scholes option pricing, real options methodology, VAR (value at risk), and risk assessment frameworks.

courses.name: Applied Analytics in the Life Sciences
courses.description: Capstone course integrating key elements from the BIA program. Students apply learned concepts, principles, and methods to authentic challenges within their chosen application domain. Culminates in a poster session where students present projects for feedback from industry practitioners in pharmaceutical and life sciences.

degree_requirements.minimum_grade: B average required across all courses

engineering_tools_and_methods.note: Select 1 course from the Engineering Tools and Methods pool (ME 564, 594, 635, 641, or 651)

robotics_core.note: Select 3 courses from the M.E. Robotics Core pool (ME 598, 621, 650, 655, or 656)

me_robotics_electives.note: At least 2 courses from ME Robotics Elective pool

cs_ee_cpe_ma_electives.note: At least 2 courses from CS, EE, CPE, or MA elective pool

flexible.note: Remaining 2 courses from any elective pool, or from School of Engineering and Science (may be outside listed offerings with advisor approval)

special_rules.me_702_practicum: ME 702 counts as an elective only if taken three times (3 credits total); a single enrollment counts for extra credit beyond the required 30
special_rules.thesis_or_project: Students may substitute ME 800 (Special Problems, 3 cr) or ME 900 (Thesis, 3 cr) for an equivalent number of elective courses

engineering_tools_and_methods.description: Engineering Tools and Methods Courses — may count as ME Robotics Elective if not used for core requirement

courses.name: Optimization Principles in Mechanical Engineering
courses.typically_offered: Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Summer Session 1
courses.description: Application of mathematical optimization techniques, including linear and nonlinear methods, to design and manufacture of devices and systems of interest to mechanical engineers. Optimization techniques include constrained and unconstrained optimization in several variables, problems for structured multi-stage decision, and linear programming. Formulation of design and manufacturing problems using computer-based methods; optimum design of parts and assemblies to minimize the cost of manufacture.
courses.prerequisites: Graduate Student or At Least Junior

courses.name: Numerical Methods in Mechanical Engineering
courses.description: Numerical methods applied to mechanical engineering problems.

courses.name: Modeling and Simulation
courses.description: Modeling and simulation techniques for mechanical engineering systems.

courses.name: Engineering Analysis I
courses.description: Advanced engineering analysis methods and mathematical techniques for mechanical engineering applications.

courses.name: Analytic Dynamics
courses.description: Advanced dynamics using analytical mechanics methods including Lagrangian and Hamiltonian formulations.

courses.name: Introduction to Robotics
courses.description: Foundational course covering robotics fundamentals including kinematics, dynamics, sensing, actuation, and control of robotic systems.

courses.name: Introduction to Modern Control Engineering
courses.description: Modern control theory and its application to engineering systems. State-space methods, stability analysis, feedback design, and digital control.

courses.name: Robot Manipulators
courses.description: Analysis and design of robot manipulators. Topics include forward and inverse kinematics, workspace analysis, trajectory planning, and motion control of robotic arms.

courses.name: Wearable Robotics and Sensors
courses.description: Design and analysis of wearable robotic systems and sensor technologies. Applications include exoskeletons, prosthetics, and human-robot interaction devices.

courses.name: Autonomous Navigation for Mobile Robots
courses.description: Methods and algorithms for autonomous navigation of mobile robots. Topics include localization, mapping, SLAM, path planning, and obstacle avoidance.

me_robotics_electives.description: Mechanical Engineering Robotics Elective Courses — choose at least 2

courses.name: Mechatronics
courses.description: Integration of mechanical, electrical, and computer systems. Topics include sensors, actuators, microcontrollers, signal conditioning, and system integration for electromechanical devices.

courses.name: Microprocessor Applications in Mechanical Engineering
courses.description: Application of microprocessors and embedded systems to mechanical engineering problems. Programming, interfacing, and real-time control.

courses.name: Robust and Adaptive Control for Dynamical Systems
courses.description: Advanced control techniques for dynamical systems with uncertainty. Covers robust control design, adaptive algorithms, and their application to robotic and mechanical systems.

courses.name: Mechanical Vibrations
courses.description: Analysis of vibrating mechanical systems. Single and multi-degree-of-freedom systems, free and forced response, vibration isolation, and modal analysis.

courses.name: Mobile Microrobotic Systems
courses.description: Design, fabrication, and control of mobile microrobotic systems. Covers actuation mechanisms, power delivery, sensing, and locomotion at the micro scale.

courses.name: Practicum in Mechanical Engineering
courses.description: Practical engineering experience in mechanical engineering. Counts as an elective course if taken three times; otherwise counts for extra credit beyond the required 30 credits.

courses.name: Special Problems in Mechanical Engineering
courses.description: Independent project course in mechanical engineering. Students may substitute this for the appropriate number of regular elective courses.

courses.name: Thesis in Mechanical Engineering
courses.description: Thesis research in mechanical engineering. Students may substitute this for the appropriate number of regular elective courses.

cs_ee_cpe_ma_electives.description: Computer Science, Electrical/Computer Engineering, and Mathematical Sciences Elective Courses — choose at least 2

courses.name: 3D Computer Vision
courses.description: Three-dimensional computer vision techniques including stereo vision, structure from motion, 3D reconstruction, and depth sensing.

courses.name: Artificial Intelligence
courses.description: Fundamental concepts and techniques in artificial intelligence including search, knowledge representation, reasoning, planning, and machine learning.

courses.name: Computer Vision
courses.description: Computational methods for interpreting visual information. Topics include image formation, feature detection, segmentation, object recognition, and scene understanding.

courses.name: Machine Learning: Fundamentals and Applications
courses.description: Fundamental machine learning algorithms and their practical applications. Covers supervised and unsupervised learning, model evaluation, and real-world deployment.

courses.name: Statistical Machine Learning
courses.description: Statistical foundations of machine learning. Covers probabilistic models, Bayesian methods, statistical inference, and learning theory.

courses.name: Introduction to Programming, Data Structures, and Algorithms
courses.description: Foundational programming concepts, data structures, and algorithmic problem solving for graduate students.

courses.name: Deep Learning
courses.description: Deep neural network architectures and training methods. Covers CNNs, RNNs, transformers, and their applications in computer vision and natural language processing.

courses.name: Algorithms
courses.description: Advanced algorithm design and analysis. Topics include sorting, graph algorithms, dynamic programming, NP-completeness, and approximation algorithms.

courses.name: Autonomous Mobile Robotic Systems
courses.description: Design and implementation of autonomous mobile robotic systems. Covers perception, planning, and control for ground and aerial robots.

courses.name: Applied Machine Learning
courses.description: Practical machine learning methods and tools applied to engineering problems. Hands-on experience with real datasets and industry-relevant applications.

courses.name: Engineering Programming: Python
courses.description: Python programming for engineering applications. Covers scientific computing, data analysis, simulation, and engineering problem solving with Python.

courses.name: Engineering Programming: C++
courses.description: C++ programming for engineering applications. Covers object-oriented design, memory management, performance optimization, and real-time systems programming.

courses.name: Nonlinear Control
courses.description: Analysis and design of nonlinear control systems. Covers Lyapunov stability, feedback linearization, sliding mode control, and applications to robotic systems.

courses.name: Cooperating Autonomous Mobile Robots
courses.description: Multi-robot systems and cooperative autonomy. Topics include distributed sensing, communication, coordination algorithms, and swarm robotics.

courses.name: Optimal Control Theory
courses.description: Mathematical theory of optimal control. Covers Pontryagin's maximum principle, Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation, and applications to engineering systems.

courses.name: Dynamic Programming and Reinforcement Learning
courses.description: Dynamic programming principles and reinforcement learning algorithms. Covers Bellman equations, value iteration, policy gradient methods, and applications to sequential decision making.

MS_Applied_Artificial_Intelligence.department: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
concentrations: Artificial Intelligence in Design and Construction

mathematical_foundation.note: Select 1 course: EE 602 (Analytical Methods) OR EE 605 (Probability and Stochastic Processes I)

core.note: Select 4 courses from the core pool (AAI 595, AAI 646, EE 608, AAI 627, AAI 628, AAI 672)

concentration.note: Select 3 courses from one of the 8 concentration areas

components: 1 elective at 500/600 level (3 credits, from department or external with advisor approval)

degree_requirements.notes: Electives in the project track can be from the department or external courses with advisor approval

courses.name: Analytical Methods in Electrical Engineering
courses.description: Advanced mathematical methods for electrical engineering including linear algebra, differential equations, transform methods, and numerical analysis.

courses.name: Probability and Stochastic Processes I
courses.description: Probability theory and stochastic processes for engineering applications. Covers random variables, distributions, expectation, and introduction to random processes.

courses.name: Applied Machine Learning
courses.description: Practical machine learning methods including supervised and unsupervised learning, model selection, regularization, and deployment for real-world engineering applications.

courses.name: Pattern Recognition and Classification
courses.description: Statistical and machine learning approaches to pattern recognition and classification. Covers Bayesian classifiers, discriminant analysis, neural networks, and performance evaluation.

courses.name: Applied Modeling and Optimization
courses.description: Mathematical modeling and optimization techniques applied to engineering systems. Covers linear programming, nonlinear optimization, and convex optimization with engineering applications.

courses.name: Data Acquisition, Modeling and Analysis: Big Data Analytics
courses.description: Big data analytics methods for large-scale data acquisition, processing, and analysis. Covers distributed computing frameworks, data pipelines, and scalable machine learning.

courses.name: Data Acquisition, Modeling and Analysis: Deep Learning
courses.description: Deep learning for data acquisition and modeling. Covers neural network architectures, training techniques, and applications to real-world datasets.

courses.name: Applied Game Theory and Evolutionary Algorithms
courses.description: Game theory principles and evolutionary computation applied to AI problems. Covers Nash equilibria, multi-agent systems, genetic algorithms, and optimization.

courses.name: Digital Signal Processing
courses.description: Digital signal processing theory and applications. Covers discrete-time signals, Z-transform, DFT, FFT, FIR/IIR filter design, and spectral analysis.

courses.name: Introduction to Control Theory
courses.description: Fundamentals of feedback control systems. Covers transfer functions, time and frequency domain analysis, stability criteria, and classical controller design.

courses.name: Wireless Networking: Architectures, Protocols and Standards
courses.description: Wireless network architectures, protocols, and standards including WiFi, cellular, and IoT communication systems.

courses.name: Linear System Theory
courses.description: State-space analysis of linear dynamical systems. Covers controllability, observability, stability, and state feedback design.

courses.name: Applied Modeling and Optimization
courses.description: Mathematical modeling and optimization techniques applied to engineering systems.

courses.name: Communication Theory
courses.description: Principles of digital and analog communications. Covers modulation, detection, channel capacity, coding, and noise analysis.

courses.name: Digital and Computer Systems Architecture
courses.description: Computer architecture principles including processor design, memory hierarchy, pipelining, and parallel processing.

courses.name: Real-Time and Embedded Systems
courses.description: Design and analysis of real-time and embedded systems. Covers scheduling, operating systems, hardware-software co-design, and reliability.

courses.name: Applied Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.description: Data structures and algorithms for engineering applications. Covers trees, graphs, hashing, sorting, and algorithm design paradigms.

courses.name: Computer and Information Networks
courses.description: Computer networking principles including TCP/IP, routing, switching, network security, and modern network architectures.

courses.name: Introduction to VLSI Design
courses.description: VLSI circuit design methodologies including CMOS logic, layout, simulation, and design automation tools.

courses.name: Applied Modeling and Optimization
courses.description: Mathematical modeling and optimization techniques applied to engineering systems.

courses.name: Engineering Programming: Python
courses.description: Python programming for AI and data engineering applications. Covers data manipulation, scientific computing, and machine learning libraries.

courses.name: Data Acquisition, Modeling and Analysis: Big Data Analytics
courses.description: Big data analytics methods for large-scale data acquisition, processing, and analysis.

courses.name: Data Acquisition, Modeling and Analysis: Deep Learning
courses.description: Deep learning for data acquisition and modeling.

courses.name: Applied Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.description: Data structures and algorithms for engineering applications.

courses.name: Engineering Programming: Python
courses.description: Python programming for AI and software engineering applications.

courses.name: Engineering Programming: Java
courses.description: Java programming for engineering applications. Covers object-oriented design, data structures, and software engineering principles.

courses.name: Engineering Programming: C++
courses.description: C++ programming for engineering applications including performance-critical and systems software.

courses.name: Data Acquisition, Modeling and Analysis: Big Data Analytics
courses.description: Big data analytics methods for large-scale data acquisition, processing, and analysis.

courses.name: Data Acquisition, Modeling and Analysis: Deep Learning
courses.description: Deep learning for data acquisition and modeling.

courses.name: Applied Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.description: Data structures and algorithms for engineering applications.

courses.name: Special Topics in Computer Engineering
courses.description: Special topics in computer engineering covering emerging areas at the frontier of the field.

courses.name: Special Topics in Biomedical Engineering
courses.description: Special topics in biomedical engineering covering emerging areas in medical technology and bioengineering.

courses.name: Introduction to Brain-Machine Interfaces
courses.description: Principles and applications of brain-machine interfaces. Covers neural signal acquisition, processing, decoding, and feedback for neuroprosthetics.

courses.name: Medical Instrumentation and Imaging
courses.description: Medical instrumentation design and imaging modalities including MRI, CT, ultrasound, and optical imaging systems.

courses.name: Medical Instrumentation and Imaging
courses.description: Medical instrumentation and imaging from a computer engineering perspective, covering signal processing and system design.

courses.name: Molecular Genetics
courses.description: Advanced molecular genetics covering gene expression, regulation, genomics, and applications to computational biology.

courses.name: Biochemistry I - Cellular Metabolism and Regulation
courses.description: Biochemical pathways and regulation of cellular metabolism including enzyme kinetics, metabolic networks, and energy transduction.

courses.name: Computational Biology
courses.description: Computational approaches to biological problems including sequence analysis, structural biology, systems biology, and bioinformatics.

courses.name: Introduction to Robotics
courses.description: Foundational course covering robotics fundamentals including kinematics, dynamics, sensing, actuation, and control.

courses.name: Introduction to Modern Control Engineering
courses.description: Modern control theory and its application to engineering systems including state-space methods and feedback design.

courses.name: Computer-Integrated Design and Manufacturing
courses.description: Computer-aided design and manufacturing systems. Covers CAD/CAM, CNC machining, rapid prototyping, and digital manufacturing.

courses.name: Urban Oceanography
courses.description: Oceanography principles applied to urban coastal environments, covering fluid dynamics, environmental impacts, and coastal engineering.

courses.name: Leading Construction Organizations
courses.description: Leadership and management principles for construction organizations including project management, team dynamics, and organizational strategy.

courses.name: Strategic Responses to Cyclical Environments
courses.description: Strategic management in cyclical industries. Covers market analysis, risk management, and adaptive strategies for construction firms.

courses.name: Sustainable Design
courses.description: Sustainable design principles and practices in construction. Covers green building standards, life cycle assessment, and environmental impact reduction.

courses.name: Project Course
courses.description: Capstone project course for MS Applied AI students. Students complete an applied AI project demonstrating mastery of program competencies.

courses.name: Research Seminar
courses.description: Research seminar co-requisite for thesis track students. Covers research methodology, scientific communication, and emerging topics in electrical and computer engineering.

courses.name: Thesis Courses
courses.description: Graduate thesis research in applied artificial intelligence. Students conduct original research under faculty supervision and produce a thesis.
